name: Vía Verde Agost – Maigmó and Tibi pass: loop from Sant Vicent del Raspeig
name_original: Vía Verde Agost - Maigmó y puerto de Tibi — Circular desde Sant Vicent del Raspeig
region: Sant Vicent del Raspeig / San Vicente del Raspeig
description:
Trails through desert landscapes, paths between vineyards and orchards, mountain landscapes, ravines and beach, this demanding route has it all. The route is circular and goes from the San Vicente del Raspeig train station towards the Maigmó mountain range, riding along cycle paths, agricultural roads and the Agost - Maigmó Greenway in a constant, but gentle, climb.

Almost halfway along the route you find yourself in Tibi, an ideal town for a stop that allows you, if you want, to recharge your batteries. Next you have a short, but slightly more demanding climb, which includes a detour to crown the Tibi pass by road. If you prefer to continue, the agricultural landscape of the Monnegre valley awaits you, whose slopes are covered with terraced orchards where almond trees, olive trees, citrus trees and vegetables thrive.

Little by little, you will make your way through small streets in the more urbanised areas of the coast until you reach the beach of San Juan, ideal for a swim. You will continue along coastal cycle paths to Alicante and cross it on its friendly network of cycle paths until you return to the San Vicente del Raspeig station. Before leaving, you can take advantage of the opportunity to treat yourself in any of its bars or restaurants.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Sant Vicent del Raspeig / San Vicente del Raspeig offers access to varied terrain, transitioning from urban settings to natural landscapes. The region is situated near the Serra del Maigmó and Serra del Sit mountain ranges, providing elevation changes and natural routes suitable for gravel bikes. Rural roads with good surfaces and comfortable tracks connect these areas, offering diverse options for exploration. This proximity to mountainous surroundings and a network of trails defines the gravel biking experience in the area. Best gravel bike trails around Sant Vicent del Raspeig / San Vicente del Raspeig The most popular gravel bike trail is Vía Verde Agost – Maigmó and Tibi pass: loop from Sant Vicent del Raspeig, a difficult 53.9 miles (86.7 km) trail that takes 6 hours 18 minutes to complete. This route incorporates a greenway and mountain passes, offering both scenic views and challenging climbs. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is First tunnel from Agost – Maigmó Greenway loop from Cargador EV Wenea, a moderate 15.4 miles (24.8 km) path. This route follows a greenway, passing through tunnels and offering a mix of paved and unpaved segments. Local gravel bikers also love the Via Verde de Alcoy – L'Estepar Rest Area loop from Restaurante Maigmó, a 78.5 miles (126.4 km) trail leading through the Vía Verde and towards a rest area, often completed in about 8 hours 43 minutes. Gravel biking around Sant Vicent del Raspeig / San Vicente del Raspeig is defined by mountainous surroundings, rural roads, and natural routes, including greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult mountain loops. The routes in Sant Vicent del Raspeig / San Vicente del Raspeig are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 500 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Sant Vicent del Raspeig / San Vicente del Raspeig's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available in Sant Vicent del Raspeig / San Vicente del Raspeig?
A: There are over 60 gravel bike trails around Sant Vicent del Raspeig / San Vicente del Raspeig, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of gravel biking in this region?
A: The gravel biking trails in Sant Vicent del Raspeig / San Vicente del Raspeig are predominantly challenging, with 44 routes classified as difficult. There are also 16 moderate routes and a few easier options available, ensuring there's something for every experience level.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails around Sant Vicent del Raspeig?
A: You can expect a diverse range of terrain, from rural roads with good surfaces and comfortable tracks to more rugged nature trails in the nearby Serra del Maigmó and Serra del Sit mountain ranges. Many routes incorporate greenways, offering a mix of paved and unpaved segments suitable for gravel bikes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly gravel biking options in Sant Vicent del Raspeig?
A: While many routes are challenging, there are some options that might be suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. The greenways, such as sections of the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23746170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;First tunnel from Agost – Maigmó Greenway loop from Cargador EV Wenea&lt;/a&gt;, offer smoother surfaces and a more relaxed experience. Always check the route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see along the gravel bike trails?
A: The region offers a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore areas near the Serra del Maigmó and Serra del Sit mountain ranges, known for their scenic trails. Some routes might pass by highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2963496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rabasa Lagoons&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2967800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artificial Lake and Waterfall at El Palmeral Park&lt;/a&gt;. For historical interest, the wider area features castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/607512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Bárbara Castle&lt;/a&gt;, though these might require a slight detour from the main gravel routes.

Q: Are there any circular gravel bike routes available in the area?
A: Yes, many gravel bike routes in Sant Vicent del Raspeig / San Vicente del Raspeig are designed as loops. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1922594431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Agost – Maigmó and Tibi pass: loop from Sant Vicent del Raspeig&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23746170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;First tunnel from Agost – Maigmó Greenway loop from Cargador EV Wenea&lt;/a&gt; are both circular routes, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy the most about gravel biking in Sant Vicent del Raspeig / San Vicente del Raspeig?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 40 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain that transitions seamlessly from urban settings to natural landscapes, the well-maintained rural roads, and the challenging yet rewarding climbs in the mountainous surroundings.

Q: Can I access the Vía Verde de Maigmó by gravel bike from Sant Vicent del Raspeig?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde de Maigmó is a key feature of the local gravel biking network. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1922594431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Agost – Maigmó and Tibi pass: loop from Sant Vicent del Raspeig&lt;/a&gt; directly incorporate sections of this greenway, making it accessible for gravel bikers starting from Sant Vicent del Raspeig.

Q: What is the best season for gravel biking in Sant Vicent del Raspeig?
A: The Valencia and Alicante region, including Sant Vicent del Raspeig, is recognized as a year-round destination for cyclists. However, for gravel biking, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures. Summers can be very hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months. Winters are mild, making it a good option for escaping colder climates.

Q: Are there any long-distance gravel routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a significant challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23743128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de Alcoy – L'Estepar Rest Area loop from Restaurante Maigmó&lt;/a&gt; cover over 126 km and involve substantial elevation gain, typically taking over 8 hours to complete. These routes delve deep into the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Is it possible to combine gravel biking with views of iconic landmarks like Puig Campana?
A: Yes, some routes offer stunning panoramic views of the surrounding landscape, including distant landmarks. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40013427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Puig Campana – Maigmó and Sit Mountains loop from Sant Vicent del Raspeig&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to provide vistas of Puig Campana while exploring the Maigmó and Sit Mountains.

Q: What are the options for finding refreshments or rest areas along the trails?
A: While many routes venture into natural areas, some are designed to pass by or lead to facilities. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23743128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de Alcoy – L'Estepar Rest Area loop from Restaurante Maigmó&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads towards a rest area. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer routes, and check the route details for any specific points of interest or facilities.
tips: The road is very entertaining and is in good condition. The views towards the Campo de Alicante are very beautiful.

What a fantastic region! With my newfound knowledge, I see this magnificent landscape in a completely different light! The landscape is typically Mediterranean, appearing rather dry and rocky. Pine forests grow on the lower slopes of the mountains, while higher up you'll find mostly low shrubs and hardy plants.

https://www.comunitatvalenciana.com/de/alacant-alicante/tibi/naturlandschaften/das-maigmo-massiv-und-die-sierra-de-l-arguenya

The port of Tibi exceeds 700 meters above sea level. It is more than two kilometers long with slopes of up to 12%.

It is a relatively short port compared to the surrounding ports. Do not underestimate it, as the steep slopes can catch you by surprise.

From the road you can see a good stretch of the Les Salines ravine. The water has eroded the limestone for thousands of years, revealing the sediment substrates that in this area have different colors. This creates a spectacular landscape of reddish, gray and bluish stone tones that contrasts with the vegetation.

The views from this point on the Montnegre road will not leave you indifferent. From here you can see a good part of the valley flanked by peaks and limestone slopes dotted with the greenery of the vegetation.
The beach of Sant Joan is very long and has white sand. Towards the north you can see the impressive mountains that rise above the coast.

waypoints.name: Views of the Les Salines ravine
